frameworks.name: Bayesian posterior inference
frameworks.one_line: Perceptual judgment as posterior combining noisy likelihood with prior over stimulus distribution.

frameworks.name: noisy cognition
frameworks.one_line: Sensory noise drives both bias toward distribution mean and response variability.

frameworks.name: subjective confidence as posterior uncertainty
frameworks.one_line: Confidence reports operationalized as a readout of posterior precision.

core_question: How does subjective confidence relate to the central tendency bias and response variability in perceptual magnitude estimation?
key_assumptions: Observer combines prior over stimulus distribution with noisy likelihood via Bayes rule.
Subjective confidence reflects posterior uncertainty about the judgment.
Sensory noise can be exogenously manipulated to shift posterior precision.
manipulations: Objective stimulus magnitude across the distribution.
Multiple data sets / perceptual magnitude tasks.
key_findings: Lower confidence predicts reduced sensitivity of estimates to objective stimuli.
Lower confidence predicts higher sensitivity to the stimulus distribution mean.
Central tendency effect strengthens at higher stimulus magnitudes when confidence is lower.
Lower confidence predicts higher response variability.
interpretations: Subjective confidence functions as a behavioral signature of posterior uncertainty driving central tendency.
Noisy-cognition Bayesian account unifies bias and variability via a single uncertainty variable.
